title: Present simple: everyday verbs and 'be'
lesson: In the present simple, the verb stays the same except with he, she, or it, where we add -s (work → works). Verbs ending in -o, -ch, -sh, -s, or -x add -es (go → goes, watch → watches). The verb 'be' is special: I am, he/she/it is, you/we/they are.

items.explanation: With 'I' the verb has no -s: I work.
With 'she', verbs ending in -o add -es: go → goes.
With 'he', verbs ending in -ch add -es: watch → watches.
With 'we' the verb has no -s: we play.
With 'my sister' (= she) the verb adds -s: speaks.
The verb 'be' with 'I' is 'am': I am a student.
The verb 'be' with 'he' is 'is': He is my best friend.
The verb 'be' with 'they' is 'are': They are at home.

items.text: The baby {1} a lot at night.
items.explanation: With 'the baby' (= it), a consonant + y becomes -ies: cry → cries.
With 'you' the verb has no -s: you drink.

items.text: My father {1} a car to work.
items.explanation: With 'my father' (= he) the verb adds -s: drives.
With 'we' the verb has no -s: we have. (Note: with he/she/it, 'have' becomes 'has'.)
